demographics.living_situation: lives independently; works as a heavy manual labourer

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My groin is completely raw and burning. It itches so badly I can't stop scratching, and now it hurts too much to even walk or wear underwear.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A red, itchy rash in the creases of my groin has been there for weeks, but in the last few days it has flared up badly, gone raw and burning, and the pain when my thighs rub together is stopping me walking properly.

symptom_narrative.onset: an itchy red rash in both groin creases started about three weeks ago; it flared up dramatically over the last four days
symptom_narrative.duration: about three weeks overall, with a sudden severe worsening in the last four days
symptom_narrative.character: well-defined red scaly patches in the groin creases with a raised, flaky, ring-like edge that is slowly spreading down the inner thighs; the middle of the patch has gone a brownish colour; over the last few days it turned raw, angry-red, burning and a bit weepy in the deepest creases
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the itch is unbearable and the skin now burns and stings; the pain when the thighs rub together is bad enough that I walk with my legs apart and can't wear normal underwear
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: both groin creases, spreading down onto the inner thighs; the rash does not involve the scrotum or penis
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant itch and burning, much worse with sweating and with friction from walking or tight clothing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing has really helped; keeping the legs apart reduces the rubbing pain a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: sweating at work, tight synthetic underwear, and the last few days it got dramatically worse after I started putting a strong cream on it
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no fever, no shivers or feeling unwell in myself, no thick yellow pus, and no hot spreading swelling beyond the rash — just the raw burning skin and the itch
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: have had itchy groin and 'athlete's foot' type problems on and off before, but never this raw or painful
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: walking with a wide-based gait to avoid friction; struggling to work and to wear underwear because of the pain

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: recurrent itchy skin in the groin and feet over the years

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a strong steroid cream a friend gave me (it was their prescription eczema cream) — I've been putting it on the groin rash for the last 4 days

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: works as a heavy manual labourer and sweats a lot during the day; usually wears tight synthetic athletic underwear

objective_data_offered.value: well-demarcated red scaly patches in both groin creases with a raised, flaky, arc-shaped advancing edge that is spreading about 10 cm down the inner thighs; the centre of each patch is clearer and a brownish colour; the scrotum and penis are not involved
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described from a video/photo view of the area, not a clinician skin examination, dermoscopy or skin-scraping; the depth of any skin breakdown cannot be judged remotely

objective_data_offered.value: over the last few days the skin has become very red, raw and angry-looking, with slight weeping/moistness deep in the creases; no thick yellow or green pus is visible
uncomfortable, shifting weight and keeping the legs apart, but alert, well in himself and not looking unwell or feverish
36.9 degrees Celsius on a home thermometer
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-measured on a home thermometer and self-reported; not a clinician-verified reading

objective_data_offered.value: reports no fever, no chills or rigors, no rapidly spreading hot swelling beyond the rash and no foul-smelling discharge
